newsx.moreLead: Background from production and material science — click to expand.

posts.teaser: KWT GmbH’s ISO management system now covers quality, environment and energy — DAkkS-accredited.
body: Since October 2025, KWT GmbH’s management system is triple certified: ISO 9001:2015 for quality, ISO 14001:2015 for environment and ISO 50001:2018 for energy. The certificates are issued by a DAkkS-accredited certification body and are valid until October 2028.

For procurement and compliance this means documented, auditable processes from extruder to dispatch. The lived GENAU management system already met the requirements of DIN EN ISO 14001 and 50001 — certification makes it externally robust.
Both certificate versions (DE/EN) are available for download; bundle the full audit package in the Trust Center.

posts.t: Continuously marked: traceability from the extruder
posts.teaser: Every K-Aqua pipe carries continuous marking applied directly in the extrusion process.
body: At up to 14,000 metres of pipe per day and line, traceability is not optional: every pipe is continuously marked during extrusion — with the information specifiers and inspectors need on site.

The marking links the physical product to its batch and the QA test records. What is printed on the pipe can be traced back to the test lab — one building block of the QA manual that auditors can request in the Trust Center.

posts.teaser: From flare gas to drinking-water pipe: the short material history of polypropylene.
body: Polypropylene is made from gases that used to be flared off uselessly — using them as a material cuts air pollution at the source. As an engineering plastic, PP is unusually resistant to solvents, alkalis and acids and forms complex geometries in injection moulding.

Inside the pipe, the bore is what counts: at 0.007 mm roughness, pressure loss stays minimal and biofilm and incrustation find little hold. Add thermal conductivity of 0.24 W/(m·K) — hot water stays hot without the line working against itself.
PP-RCT pushes the limits further: the modified crystalline structure brings more temperature and pressure reserve — SDR 7.4 sustains 70 °C at 10 bar continuously.

posts.t: Brass meets polypropylene: transitions into existing stock
posts.teaser: Renovation almost always means a system change — transition pieces with brass threads make it clean.
body: Hardly any project starts on a greenfield: existing copper or galvanised-steel lines must be connected. Transition pieces with embedded brass female or male threads do the job — the thread side connects the metal stock, the PP side is fusion-welded.

This enables section-by-section renovation: corroded runs are replaced, working ones remain — and the corrosion problem does not migrate into the new system. Unions keep the connection detachable where needed, e.g. at valves and water meters.

newsx.ishText: The building-services industry meets at ISH in Frankfurt — the world’s leading trade fair where partner KESSEL regularly shows presence. As soon as K-Aqua dates are fixed, they will appear here.
